meta.venue: Curated readings in robotics and AI

items.title: Fine-Tuning Vision-Language-Action Models: Optimizing Speed and Success
items.authors: Moo Jin Kim, Chelsea Finn, Percy Liang
items.description: Introduces OpenVLA-OFT with key improvements including parallel action chunking, multi-image inputs via FiLM, and enhanced action generation. Achieves significantly faster inference gains.
keyIdeaToolbox: Action chunking and parallel action decoding for faster inference instead of diffusion
L1 loss with continuous actions and action chunking performs better than diffusion action generation
FiLM (Feature-wise Linear Modulation) to implement affine transformations for multi-image inputs across image patches
Continuous action representation instead of discrete actions

items.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
items.authors: Edward J. Hu, Yelong Shen, Phillip Wallis, Zeyuan Allen-Zhu, Yuanzhi Li, Shean Wang, Lu Wang, Weizhu Chen
items.description: Proposes Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), which freezes pre-trained model weights and injects trainable low-rank matrices into each Transformer layer. Reduces trainable parameters by 10,000x and GPU memory by 3x compared to full fine-tuning, while achieving comparable or better performance on RoBERTa, DeBERTa, GPT-2, and GPT-3. For linear layers with weight matrix W of shape (d_out, d_in), LoRA decomposes updates into trainable matrices B (shape r × d_in) and A (shape d_out × r), where r is a small rank. During inference, the A*B product is added to original weights, enabling efficient adaptation with minimal overhead; r can be as low as 1.
keyIdeaToolbox: Universally applicable to linear layers in any architecture

items.title: FiLM: Visual Reasoning with a General Conditioning Layer
items.authors: Ethan Perez, Florian Strub, Harm de Vries, Vincent Dumoulin, Aaron Courville
items.description: Introduces Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM), a general-purpose conditioning method that applies feature-wise affine transformations to neural network computation. FiLM layers modulate features based on conditioning information, enabling effective visual reasoning.
keyIdeaToolbox: Affine transformations of image patches directly: y = γ ⊙ x + β

items.title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
items.authors: Cheng Chi, Zhenjia Xu, Siyuan Feng, Eric Cousineau, Yilun Du, Benjamin Burchfiel, Russ Tedrake, Shuran Song
items.description: Introduces Diffusion Policy, which represents robot visuomotor policies as conditional denoising diffusion processes.
keyIdeaToolbox: Diffusion models for action generation and policy learning

items.title: C++ Templates: The Complete Guide
items.author: David Vandevoorde, Nicolai M. Josuttis, Douglas Gregor
items.description: The book has 3 parts. Part 1 is a must for anyone doing any serious C++ metaprogramming. I would recommend every C++ programmer to read Part 1. C++ templates are a programming language within the C++ language which means syntax might not always be the first thing it is optimized on. Also, resist the urge to do everything in compile time!

items.title: Mastering PyTorch
items.author: Ashish Ranjan Jha
items.description: This book isn't just about PyTorch. It's a great book to learn about different deep learning model architectures.

items.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Hands-On
items.author: Maxim Lapan
items.description: I've read a lot of texts for RL but this one was practical, clear, and easy to understand. Prerequisite would be an introduction to RL course. It has many examples and is easy to follow. It is for practitioners, not theorists, which is great if you're building things.
